citation.description: require_authenticated = false confirmed in rebar.toml; ticket comment records Gerrit change 777 MERGED 00:23:06 and CI unblocked.

criteria.criterion: Enforcement reverted to advisory on main; verify-identity job green on a fresh change.

citation.description: Ticket comments contain detailed, specific root-cause analysis: (1) 206 compacted SNAPSHOT ledger entries with commit_sha=NULL causing key_not_valid_at_era via topology-fragile per-entry resolver; (2) COMPLETION_VERDICT sidecar class falsified as a co-cause — unsigned sidecars dropped at KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES filter. Two independent investigators converged; deterministic proof (force commit_sha=None -> key_not_valid_at_era; resolve real commit -> verified). Mechanism cites verify_authorship.py line numbers and exact CI counts.

criteria.criterion: Proven, cited root cause for BOTH failure classes (why the 4cc3ffbbb fixes did not hold post-re-enablement).

citation.description: Two concrete non-stub regression tests exist: test_archived_ticket_out_of_scope (defect 1: archived scope) and test_unresolvable_null_commit_ledger_does_not_fail_gate (defect 2: unresolvable null-commit ledger). Both verify observable outcomes (JSON report verdicts, exit codes). Ticket comments confirm RED-first discipline with mutation check.

criteria.criterion: RED->GREEN regression test per class before any re-enablement.

citation.description: Re-enablement has NOT occurred (rebar.toml line 97: require_authenticated = false). The precondition is intact: ticket comments document 0 enforced-not-verified in all 3 environments (local store-check, GitHub Actions run 29632050718 exit 0 '0 key-not-valid-at-era', AWS review-bot deployment) as ready evidence for the user's re-enable decision. The criterion constrains re-enablement, which was deliberately withheld per user direction.

criteria.criterion: Re-enablement only after 'rebar verify-identity' reports 0 enforced-not-verified whole-store.
